safety_note: This manifest stores field names, offsets, local source anchors, and evidence status only; it does not contain ROM-derived payload bytes.

battle-action-pp-cost-probe.local_field: BATTLE_ACTION_TABLE row 13 cost byte at row +0x03

runtime_consumers.routine: C1:CC39 Battle PSI menu controller PP guard
runtime_consumers.detail: The controller resolves the selected D5:8A50 PSI row to its associated D5:7B68 action id, indexes the 12-byte action row, adds +0x03, and compares that PP cost byte against the acting character's current PP.
C2 action dispatch consumes the same D5:7B68 descriptor family's action pointer field at row +0x08 after target-mask construction.

enemy-insane-cultist-action1-probe.local_field: ENEMY_CONFIGURATION_TABLE row 1 normal action id slot 0 at row +0x46

runtime_consumers.detail: The controller resolves the enemy id into the D5:9589 enemy row, reads action_order at +0x45, selects a normal action slot, reads the action id from +0x46 + slot*2, and stages the action plus its argument into the battle row.
The local data contract maps +0x46 as four normal action ids and +0x50 as their paired action arguments within 0x5E-byte ENEMY_CONFIGURATION_TABLE rows.

enemy-insane-cultist-hp-probe.local_field: ENEMY_CONFIGURATION_TABLE row 1 hp word low byte at row +0x21

runtime_consumers.detail: The routine resolves selected enemy ids through D5:9589 with stride 0x5E, adds +0x21, reads the HP word, and combines it with the row +0x3A PP field while deciding whether the Magic Butterfly restore animation can proceed.
The local data contract maps ENEMY_CONFIGURATION_TABLE row +0x21 as the HP word within 0x5E-byte enemy_data rows; the CoilSnake HP probe lands at D5:9608, which is row 1 +0x21.

item-cost-probe.local_field: ITEM_CONFIGURATION_TABLE row cost word at item row +0x1A

runtime_consumers.detail: BuildShopItemTextEntry resolves an item row, adds +0x1A, reads the cost word, then calls C4:507A to print the active-window right-aligned decimal.
Indexes D5:5000 item rows with stride 0x27 and prints the fixed 0x19-byte item-name field.
Indexes the same item rows for battle-use/category byte +0x19 and action word +0x1D.

text-menu-probe.local_field: C4:9FE1 BattleMenuAndTargetPrecheckData row 2 fixed-width Auto Fight text at row +0x09

runtime_consumers.detail: The controller loads C4:9FE1 as the fixed-width battle menu text base, adds +0x20 for the Auto Fight row, and passes that pointer to C1:DDDA to build the selection menu entry.
The changed byte lands at C4:A00A, which is row 2 offset +0x09 inside the checked-in fixed-width Auto Fight text row.

map-palette-probe.local_field: lands inside MAP_DATA_TILE_ARRANGEMENT_5 after CoilSnake rebuild

map-door-first-destination-probe.coilsnake_field: map_doors.yml / first unique door / Destination X
map-door-first-destination-probe.local_field: changed byte lands in CoilSnake baseline-rebuild CF door payload at CF:000C; original verified ROM has different bytes at the same address

runtime_consumers.routine: CoilSnake baseline rebuild CF door-data payload
runtime_consumers.detail: The controlled diff proves CoilSnake's first unique map door Destination X low byte is emitted at baseline-rebuild offset 0x0F000C (CF:000C), changing 0x10 to 0x11 for 784 -> 785. The verified original ROM byte at CF:000C is not the same payload, so this is rebuild-layout evidence rather than a direct original-ROM runtime field promotion.

Local C0/C4 door probes resolve bank-CF door trigger records through C0:7477, then cache or enqueue destination pointers for movement and interaction transitions. The exact original-ROM subrecord mapping for CoilSnake's rebuilt CF:000C field remains open.

map-door-first-destination-y-probe.coilsnake_field: map_doors.yml / first unique door / Destination Y
map-door-first-destination-y-probe.local_field: changed byte lands in CoilSnake baseline-rebuild CF door payload at CF:000A; original verified ROM has different bytes at the same address

runtime_consumers.routine: CoilSnake baseline rebuild CF door-data payload
runtime_consumers.detail: The controlled diff proves CoilSnake's first unique map door Destination Y low byte is emitted at baseline-rebuild offset 0x0F000A (CF:000A). Together with Direction at CF:000B and Destination X at CF:000C, this describes CoilSnake's rebuilt record layout, not the original ROM layout at that address.
Local C0/C4 door probes consume bank-CF door destination records, but the original-ROM-to-CoilSnake-rebuild layout map remains open.

map-door-first-direction-probe.local_field: changed byte lands in CoilSnake baseline-rebuild CF door payload at CF:000B; original verified ROM has different bytes at the same address

runtime_consumers.routine: CoilSnake baseline rebuild CF door-data payload
runtime_consumers.detail: The controlled diff proves CoilSnake's first unique map door Direction byte is emitted at baseline-rebuild offset 0x0F000B (CF:000B), between Destination Y low byte and Destination X low byte in CoilSnake's rebuilt layout.
Local C0/C4 door probes consume bank-CF door destination records, but this direction byte still needs an original-ROM layout join before it can be called a local runtime field.

map-door-first-style-probe.local_field: changed byte lands in CoilSnake baseline-rebuild CF door payload at CF:000E; original verified ROM has different bytes at the same address

runtime_consumers.routine: CoilSnake baseline rebuild CF door-data payload
runtime_consumers.detail: The controlled diff proves CoilSnake's first unique map door Style byte is emitted at baseline-rebuild offset 0x0F000E (CF:000E), a few bytes after the destination/direction cluster in CoilSnake's rebuilt layout.
Local C0/C4 door probes consume bank-CF door destination records, but the rebuilt style byte still needs a pointer/runtime join back to the original ROM layout.

npc-config-first-text-pointer-probe.local_field: NPC_CONFIG_TABLE row 1 primary text/actionscript pointer low byte at row +0x09

runtime_consumers.detail: The TALK_TO selector indexes CF:8985 interaction rows as result * 0x11, adds +0x09, reads the three-byte returned pointer, and passes that output into the checking/talking pipeline.
The CHECK selector returns the same row +0x09 pointer for class 2 after installing interaction context and for class 3 directly.

The map object contract records primary and secondary text pointers for placed NPC config rows; the diff proves CoilSnake's Text Pointer 1 low byte lands in the same CF interaction row payload consumed by C1:3187 and C1:323B.

map-sprite-first-npc-id-probe.coilsnake_field: map_sprites.yml / first placed map sprite / NPC ID
map-sprite-first-npc-id-probe.local_field: changed byte lands in CoilSnake baseline-rebuild CF map-sprite payload at CF:61E9; original verified ROM uses that address as part of the sprite placement pointer table

runtime_consumers.detail: The controlled diff changes the low byte of NPC ID 1254 at baseline-rebuild offset 0x0F61E9 (CF:61E9). The verified original ROM has pointer-table bytes at the same address, so this is CoilSnake rebuilt-layout evidence rather than a direct original-ROM placement row promotion.

runtime_consumers.routine: CF:61E7 SpritePlacementPointerTable and map object bundle contracts
runtime_consumers.detail: Local contracts identify CF:61E7..CF:6BE6 as the original sprite placement pointer table and track placed-object joins separately; this probe shows CoilSnake's rebuilt map_sprites layout differs at the same address.

psi-ness-omega-level-probe.coilsnake_field: psi_ability_table.yml / ability 4 / Level learned by Ness
psi-ness-omega-level-probe.local_field: PSI_ABILITY_TABLE row 4 learn-level byte at row +0x06

runtime_consumers.detail: The category predicate walks D5:8A50 PSI rows, selects the character-specific learn-level byte (+0x06 Ness, +0x07 Paula, or +0x08 Poo), compares it against the party member's current level, and only accepts the entry when the level requirement is met.

The table note maps D5:8A50 as 15-byte PSI ability rows; the CoilSnake Ness learn-level diff at D5:8A92 lands on row 4 +0x06, matching the runtime learn-level branch used by the battle PSI category predicate.

window-config-height-probe.local_field: window configuration table row 8 height word low byte at C3:E296

runtime_consumers.detail: The diff proves CoilSnake's window 8 Height field lands at C3:E296, adjacent to the existing Width field at C3:E294. The original ROM and CoilSnake baseline rebuild match at this block, but local window-dimension consumer naming remains open.

window-config-width-probe.local_field: changed byte lands in C3:E250..C3:E3F8 preserved/event payload range after CoilSnake rebuild

runtime_consumers.detail: The diff proves CoilSnake's window width field lands in the local C3:E250..E3F8 range; local window-dimension consumer naming remains open because the current source scaffold is still coarse.
